connectShopifyStore.title: How to connect your Shopify store
connectShopifyStore.body:
To start using DisputeDesk, you need to connect your Shopify store. Here's how:

1. From the portal dashboard, click "Connect store" in the setup checklist (or use the sidebar link).
2. Enter your store's domain (e.g. my-store.myshopify.com).
3. You'll be redirected to Shopify to authorize DisputeDesk.
4. Grant the requested permissions — DisputeDesk needs access to orders, disputes, dispute evidence, fulfillments, and customer data.
5. Once authorized, you'll land on your dashboard with the connected store already active. The setup checklist will advance to the next step.

To switch between stores, click the store name in the sidebar to open the store selector. You can connect additional stores from that page.

Reconnecting a store:
If DisputeDesk has been updated with new permissions (e.g. after an app update), you may need to reconnect your store to grant the latest permissions. On the store selector page, click the "Reconnect (refresh permissions)" link below your store name. You'll be redirected to Shopify to re-authorize. Your existing data and settings are preserved — only the permissions are refreshed.

Clear shop & reconnect: In the portal sidebar, under the store name, you'll see a "Clear shop & reconnect" link. Use this if the current shop context is stuck or you want to disconnect and connect again from scratch. It clears your selected shop and sends you to the connect store page; you stay signed in with your portal account.

Your store data is encrypted and never shared with third parties.

After connecting, DisputeDesk will automatically sync your open disputes. This usually takes a few seconds.

shopifyAppStoreInstall.title: Installing from the Shopify App Store
shopifyAppStoreInstall.body:
DisputeDesk is available on the Shopify App Store. That is the recommended way to add the app to a store.

From the App Store:
1. Go to the Shopify App Store (apps.shopify.com) and search for DisputeDesk.
2. Click Add app and choose which store to install on.
3. Review and approve the permissions Shopify shows. DisputeDesk needs access to orders, disputes, dispute evidence, fulfillments, and related store data to build evidence packs.
4. After installation, open Shopify Admin, then Apps, then DisputeDesk to use the embedded app.

From our website:
If you started on disputedesk.com or another marketing page, follow the sign-up or install prompts. You will still complete Shopify OAuth. Then open DisputeDesk from Apps in Admin for billing, saving evidence, and the full embedded experience.

Tip: Opening the app from Shopify Admin (Apps, then DisputeDesk) keeps your store session reliable. If you see connection or billing errors, try Clear shop & reconnect in the portal sidebar or reopen the app from Admin. See also "Can't upgrade: Store session is invalid" in Help.

Next: read "Your first dispute sync" and "Understanding the dashboard."

understandingDashboard.title: Understanding the dashboard
understandingDashboard.body:
The dashboard gives you an at-a-glance overview of your dispute operations.

Key metrics displayed:
- Open Disputes: chargebacks currently awaiting a response.
- Win Rate: percentage of disputes resolved in your favor.
- Auto-Saved: evidence packs automatically saved to Shopify this month.
- Resolved: total disputes closed (won or lost).

The Automation Status card shows your current settings: whether auto-build and auto-save are enabled, the minimum completeness score, and whether the blocker gate is active.

The Recent Disputes table lists your latest chargebacks. The Order column links directly to the order in your Shopify Admin. Click View Details on any row to see full dispute details and manage evidence.

firstDisputeSync.title: Your first dispute sync
firstDisputeSync.body:
After connecting your store, DisputeDesk syncs your open disputes automatically.

How syncing works:
- DisputeDesk checks Shopify for new and updated disputes via the Shopify GraphQL API.
- Disputes are imported with their reason, amount, status, due date, and linked order information.
- Syncing happens automatically via webhooks when Shopify notifies us of changes.
- You can also trigger a manual sync by clicking "Sync Now" on the Disputes page.

After the first sync, if you have automation enabled, DisputeDesk will start evaluating rules and building evidence packs automatically for any disputes that need a response.

automationOverview.title: How automation works
automationOverview.body:
DisputeDesk is automation-first. Here's the flow:

1. A new dispute appears in Shopify.
2. DisputeDesk detects it (via webhook or scheduled sync).
3. Your rules are evaluated to decide: auto-pack or send to review queue.
4. If auto-pack: an evidence pack is built automatically from your order data, tracking info, and store policies.
5. The completeness score is calculated. If it meets your threshold and there are no blockers, the evidence is saved to Shopify automatically.
6. If review is required: the pack appears in your Review Queue for manual approval.

**Pack library vs routing rules:** Templates you install under Packs live in your evidence library (sections and narratives you can edit). **Automation rules** only decide whether a new dispute runs the standard auto-build pipeline or is sent to the review queue first—they do not select a library template for that automated build. Auto-build uses order, tracking, and policy collectors.

You control every step through your automation settings and rules. Automation can be fully enabled, partially enabled (auto-build but manual save), or disabled entirely.

viewingFilteringDisputes.title: Viewing and filtering disputes
viewingFilteringDisputes.body:
The Disputes page shows all your chargebacks in a sortable table.

You can filter by:
- Status: Needs response, Under review, Won, Lost.
- Search: type keywords to find specific disputes by ID, order number, or reason.

The table shows each dispute's ID, linked order, amount, reason, status, deadline, and when it was last synced.

Two tabs are available:
- All Disputes: every dispute regardless of status.
- Review Queue: only disputes flagged for manual review (based on your rules).

Click any dispute row to open the detail page where you can view evidence packs, generate new packs, or re-sync from Shopify.

syncingDisputes.title: Syncing disputes from Shopify
syncingDisputes.body:
DisputeDesk keeps your disputes up to date through two mechanisms:

1. Webhooks: Shopify sends real-time notifications when disputes are created or updated. DisputeDesk processes these instantly.
2. Manual sync: Click "Sync Now" on the Disputes page to trigger an immediate sync. This is useful if you suspect a webhook was missed.

During a sync, DisputeDesk:
- Fetches all open disputes from Shopify.
- Creates new records for any disputes not yet tracked.
- Updates existing disputes with the latest status and information.
- For new disputes, evaluates your rules and triggers automation if applicable.

Sync status is shown with a "Last synced" timestamp on each dispute.

reviewQueue.title: The review queue
reviewQueue.body:
The Review Queue contains disputes that require your manual review before evidence is saved to Shopify.

Disputes end up in the review queue when:
- A rule routes them to review (e.g. high-value disputes over a certain amount).
- No rule matches and the default action is review.
- You have "Require review before auto-save" enabled in your automation settings.

From the Review Queue tab on the Disputes page, you can:
- See all disputes awaiting your attention.
- Click into any dispute to review the evidence pack.
- Click "Approve" to trigger the automation pipeline (pack building and saving).

This gives you full control over which disputes get automated responses and which ones you want to handle personally.

approvingDisputes.title: Approving disputes from the review queue
approvingDisputes.body:
When a dispute is in your Review Queue, you need to approve it before DisputeDesk will build and save evidence.

To approve a dispute:
1. Go to the Disputes page and switch to the "Review Queue" tab.
2. Click the "Approve" button next to the dispute.
3. DisputeDesk will immediately trigger the automation pipeline.
4. An evidence pack will be built from your order data, tracking, and policies.
5. If the pack passes your completeness and blocker gates, evidence will be saved to Shopify.

You can also approve from the dispute detail page by clicking "Approve & Save."

All approvals are logged in the audit trail for compliance tracking.

howPacksBuilt.title: How evidence packs are built automatically
howPacksBuilt.body:
When DisputeDesk builds an evidence pack, it automatically collects evidence from multiple sources:

- Order data: line items, amounts, billing address (city-level only — PII is redacted), customer tenure, and refund history.
- Fulfillment & tracking: carrier name, tracking numbers, tracking URLs, and delivery status.
- Store policies: snapshots of your shipping, refund, terms, and cancellation policies.

The pack is structured by dispute reason. For example, a "Product not received" dispute will prioritize shipping and tracking evidence, while a "Fraudulent" dispute will emphasize order and customer verification.

After collection, DisputeDesk calculates a completeness score and identifies any blockers (missing required evidence). You can then upload additional files to strengthen the pack.

**Library pack templates:** Installing a template from the Packs page adds a pack to your **library** for narratives, sections, and editing. That is separate from each **automated per-dispute build**, which uses the collectors pipeline above. The automated build does not currently merge your library template structure into the pack automatically.

generatingPackManually.title: Generating a pack manually
generatingPackManually.body:
You can manually generate an evidence pack for any dispute:

1. Go to the dispute detail page.
2. Click "Generate Pack" (or "Generate New Pack" if a pack already exists).
3. DisputeDesk will collect evidence from your order, tracking, and policy data.
4. The pack will appear on the page once building is complete (usually a few seconds).

Manual generation is useful when:
- Automation is disabled and you want to create packs on demand.
- You want to regenerate a pack after uploading new evidence.
- A previous pack failed and you want to retry.

Note: pack generation counts against your monthly quota. Check your usage on the Billing page.

completenessScore.title: Understanding the completeness score
completenessScore.body:
Every evidence pack has a completeness score from 0% to 100%. This score indicates how much of the recommended evidence has been collected.

How it works:
- Each dispute reason has a template of expected evidence items (e.g. tracking info, delivery proof, policy snapshots).
- DisputeDesk checks which items are present in the pack.
- Required items contribute more weight than optional ones.
- The score is the weighted percentage of collected vs. expected items.

Scoring thresholds:
- 80%+ (green): strong pack, ready for auto-save.
- 60–79% (yellow): acceptable but could be stronger.
- Below 60% (red): weak pack — consider uploading more evidence.

If you have auto-save enabled, packs below your configured minimum score will not be saved automatically. You can adjust this threshold in Settings > Automation.

fixingBlockers.title: Fixing blockers in your evidence pack
fixingBlockers.body:
Blockers are missing required evidence items that prevent an evidence pack from being auto-saved to Shopify.

Common blockers include:
- Missing tracking information for "Product not received" disputes.
- Missing refund policy for "Credit not processed" disputes.
- Missing order details when order data could not be fetched.

To fix blockers:
1. Open the evidence pack from the dispute detail page.
2. Check the "Evidence Checklist" section — blockers are highlighted in red.
3. Upload the missing evidence using the upload section.
4. After uploading, the pack will re-evaluate and update the blocker status.

If you have "Enforce zero blockers" enabled in automation settings, packs with any remaining blockers will not be auto-saved. You can disable this gate if you prefer to save packs even with missing items.

uploadingEvidence.title: Uploading additional evidence
uploadingEvidence.body:
You can upload files to strengthen your evidence pack:

1. Open the evidence pack detail page.
2. Scroll to the "Upload Evidence" section.
3. Click to upload or drag and drop files.
4. Supported formats: images (JPG, PNG) and PDF files.
5. Maximum file size: 10 MB per file.

Uploaded files are attached to the evidence pack and included when saving to Shopify. They appear in the "Evidence Items" section with a timestamp and source label.

Common uploads include:
- Screenshots of customer communication.
- Delivery confirmation photos.
- Signed receipts or invoices.
- Additional tracking documentation.

evidenceChecklist.title: Evidence checklist explained
evidenceChecklist.body:
The Evidence Checklist shows what evidence has been collected and what's still missing.

Each item in the checklist is marked as:
- Collected (green check): this evidence has been found and included.
- Required but missing (red, labeled "required"): this is a blocker — you need to provide this evidence.
- Recommended (yellow): not strictly required, but providing it would strengthen your case.

The checklist is tailored to each dispute's reason code. For example:
- Product Not Received: prioritizes tracking, delivery proof, and shipping policy.
- Fraudulent: prioritizes order verification, customer details, and AVS/CVV match.
- Credit Not Processed: prioritizes refund records and refund policy.

Focusing on required items first will improve your completeness score and remove blockers that prevent auto-saving.

evidencePackTemplates.title: Using evidence pack templates
evidencePackTemplates.body:
The Packs page helps you get started with evidence packs using templates.

When you have no packs yet, you'll see recommended templates — pre-built packs for common dispute types (e.g. Fraudulent, Product Not Received). Each template card has an "Install" button: clicking it creates a new pack from that template for your store. After installing, you're taken to the new pack's detail page; you can also open it anytime from the Packs list. Use "Back to packs" on the pack page to return to the library.

In the embedded app (inside Shopify Admin), Evidence Packs shows these templates in a table with row actions (Activate/Edit/Delete). Use "Start from template" to install templates, then activate draft packs from the table.

Tip: If the Packs page looks blank or you can't install templates, reopen DisputeDesk from Shopify Admin (Apps > DisputeDesk) so the embedded shop session is established. DisputeDesk relies on server-side shop identity (httpOnly cookies + middleware) and can't read it from the browser.

**Template Setup Wizard:** In the portal you can use the Template Setup Wizard (Packs > customize or /portal/packs/customize) to configure a new template in four steps: choose evidence types to collect, set evidence sources (where each type comes from — auto, reusable, or manual), review how automation works and choose auto-submit or email-to-review, and activate. See "Template setup wizard" for details.

"Browse All Templates" opens the full template library so you can preview and install any template. "Create from Scratch" starts an empty pack.

Once you have at least one pack, a short banner may appear suggesting you explore more templates; you can dismiss it or click "Browse Templates" to open the library again.

Templates are a quick way to get evidence packs that match best practices; you can always edit documents and settings after installing.

**Related:** **Automation rules** (Rules page or Setup wizard) control whether new disputes are auto-built or sent to the review queue first. They do not swap in a specific library template for each automated build—see "How automation works" and "How evidence packs are built automatically."

templateSetupWizard.title: Template setup wizard
templateSetupWizard.body:
The Template Setup Wizard (in the portal at Packs > customize, or /portal/packs/customize) walks you through setting up a new evidence template in four steps.

**Step 1 — Choose evidence to collect:** Select which types of evidence this template should gather. Each type is marked Required, Recommended, or Optional, and how it is provided: Auto-collected from Shopify, Set in Policies, or You add manually. For policies (e.g. Refund policy), you'll see whether the policy is already set or "Not set — Add in Policies". You must select all required types before continuing.

**Step 2 — Set evidence sources:** Review where DisputeDesk will get each type of evidence. Each selected type is shown with a clear source: auto (pulled from your store), reusable store document (upload once in Policies; use the Upload button to add or link policies), or added manually per dispute. A summary shows how many types are Automated, Reusable, and Manual.

**Step 3 — Review how automation works:** The wizard explains the flow (dispute appears → pack prepared → ready to review). You choose how submission works: **Auto-submit to Shopify** on the dispute due date, or **Email me to review and submit** so you or your team submit manually. The "Important to know" note updates based on your choice.

**Step 4 — Activate template:** Review a summary (evidence types count, how evidence is provided, dispute type, source). You can **Save as draft** to finish later or **Activate template** to start using it. Both actions return you to the Packs library.

In the **embedded app setup** (Shopify Admin), finishing the Install flow with **Activate** creates the library pack as **ACTIVE** so it appears in the Evidence packs step and the **Automation & review** step alongside activated packs.

The sidebar shows your setup progress and a checklist of the four steps. Use "Back to Evidence Packs" at any time to leave the wizard without saving.

definingStorePolicies.title: Defining your store policies
definingStorePolicies.body:
Store policies are included automatically in evidence packs. DisputeDesk uses them to strengthen your case when responding to chargebacks.

Supported policy types: Terms of Service, Refund Policy, Shipping Policy, Privacy Policy, and Contact Information & Customer Service Policy.

The Policies page (portal) shows a Recommended Store Policies section with template cards. For each policy you can:

1. Use a template: click "Use Template" to open a starter document in your chosen language. Edit the content for your store (placeholders are prefilled where possible). You can copy, download, or click "Save & Apply" to save it as that policy type. Templates are starting points, not legal advice — review with your legal team.

2. Upload your own: if you already have a policy as a PDF or Word file, use "Upload" or "Upload Your Own" for that policy type. Accepted formats are PDF and DOCX, max 10 MB. The file is stored securely and a snapshot is used when building evidence packs.

When a policy is already defined, each row shows Edit, Preview, and Download. Use Edit to change the policy text in the same modal (for policies saved from a template); you can then save your changes with "Save & Apply." If the policy was uploaded as a file, Edit opens the template so you can replace it with editable text.

Clear all policies: To remove every policy for the store and re-review from scratch, use "Clear all policies" (top right when you have at least one policy). After confirming, all policy documents are removed; you can then add them again using templates or uploads.

Policy template language: In Settings (Policy templates section), you choose the language of the policy template text (e.g. English or Deutsch). You can use English even if your interface is in another language. This only affects the text when you open a template; it does not change your UI language.

Each policy row shows whether it's defined or not. Keeping policies up to date improves your evidence quality.

configuringAutomation.title: Dispute routing and automation settings
configuringAutomation.body:
**During setup — Automation & review step**

After you install evidence pack templates (Packs step), the **Automation & review** step explains what happens when a **new** dispute syncs from Shopify:

- **Installed library packs:** One row per template-backed pack (**draft** or **active** — not archived), with **Manual review** or **Automatic** handling. Draft packs appear as soon as you install a template, even before you activate the pack on the Packs page. Status badges distinguish active vs draft.
- **Starting presets:** Manual, Review first, or Automatic (Automatic requires installed templates). Manual is suggested for a safe first setup.
- **Default rule (General):** Fallback when no per-reason override applies — choose handling (Manual, Review first, or Auto-build) and a pack template when relevant.
- **Exceptions by reason:** Optional overrides per Shopify dispute reason (e.g. fraud, product not received), each in its own card.
- **Safeguards:** Switches for high-value review and catch-all review. When active, these **override** the default and per-reason rules when their conditions match.
- **Live summary:** A short recap of your effective choices before you save.

The **Evidence packs** step emphasizes **activated** packs in its summary; the Automation step lists **all** installed template-backed packs so you can set routing for drafts too.

You need at least one **installed** pack template before you can assign templates or use the full Automatic path; the setup screen shows a clear message if packs are missing.

**How routing is evaluated:** Amount safeguards → rule for the dispute reason → default (General) → catch-all safeguards (see technical docs: `pickAutomationAction`).

**After setup — Settings > Automation**

Store-wide toggles: auto-build evidence packs, auto-save evidence to Shopify, require review before auto-save, enforce zero blockers, and minimum completeness score (0–100, default 80) for auto-save.

**Rules page**

For custom rules with drag-and-drop order and amount ranges, use **Rules** after setup. See also "Creating your first rule" and "Rule priority."

creatingFirstRule.title: Creating your first rule
creatingFirstRule.body:
Rules let you control how disputes are routed — either to auto-pack or to the review queue.

To create a rule:
1. Go to the Rules page.
2. Click "Add Rule."
3. Fill in the details:
   - Name: a label for your reference (e.g. "High-value review").
   - Match Reasons: select which dispute reasons this rule applies to (leave empty to match all).
   - Amount range: set a minimum and/or maximum dispute amount.
   - Action: choose "Auto-Pack" (automatic processing) or "Send to Review" (manual queue).
4. Click "Create Rule."

Example: create a rule that sends all disputes over $500 to review:
- Name: "High-value review"
- Min Amount: 500
- Action: Send to Review

**What "Auto-Pack" means:** For a matching dispute, DisputeDesk may run the **standard** evidence collection pipeline when the dispute first syncs. It does **not** mean "use a specific pack template from your library"—those templates are managed under Packs separately.

Rules are available on Starter and Pro plans. Free plan users can upgrade from the Billing page.

rulePriority.title: Rule priority and evaluation order
rulePriority.body:
Rules are evaluated top to bottom. The first rule that matches a dispute wins — no further rules are checked.

How matching works:
- If a rule specifies match reasons, the dispute must have one of those reasons.
- If a rule specifies an amount range, the dispute amount must fall within that range.
- If a rule has no conditions (empty reasons, no amount range), it matches all disputes.

To reorder rules, drag and drop them on the Rules page. Put your most specific rules at the top and catch-all rules at the bottom.

If no rule matches a dispute, it goes to the Review Queue by default.

Tip: create a catch-all "Auto-Pack" rule at the bottom to automate everything, and put specific "Send to Review" rules above it for high-value or unusual disputes.

completenessBlockerGates.title: Completeness and blocker gates
completenessBlockerGates.body:
Gates are quality checks that control whether an evidence pack can be saved to Shopify.

**Automatic save gates** (automation pipeline):

1. Completeness gate: the pack's completeness score must meet your configured minimum threshold (default: 80%). Packs below this score will not be auto-saved.

2. Blocker gate: if "Enforce zero blockers" is enabled, any pack with missing required evidence items will not be auto-saved.

Both gates must pass for auto-save to proceed.

**Manual save safeguards** (Save Evidence button on the pack page):

- Empty packs (0% completeness or status "blocked") cannot be saved at all — a warning is shown and the save is blocked.
- Packs below 80% completeness show a confirmation dialog before saving. You can choose to save anyway or go back and add more evidence.
- Packs still building ("queued" or "building" status) show a spinner instead of the save button — wait until building completes.

If a gate or safeguard blocks you:
- Upload additional evidence to improve the score.
- Adjust your gate thresholds in Settings > Automation.
- For manual saves below 80%, confirm the dialog if you're satisfied with what you have.

rulePresets.title: Rule presets and suggested rules
rulePresets.body:
DisputeDesk ships four **suggested starter rules** (fraud, product not received, high-value amount, catch-all). They match the definitions in the product (`lib/rules/presets.ts`).

**Embedded app (Shopify Admin) — Rules**

The Rules screen shows **Automate Your Dispute Workflow** with those four rows. Each row has a **Routing** control: **Auto-Pack** or **Send to Review**. Click **Save starter rules** to create or update the matching rules in your store. Above that, if you have any **ACTIVE** evidence packs in your library, they are listed for context (same data as the Packs page).

**Portal — Rules**

When you have no rules (and your plan supports rules), you may see an empty state with preset cards and **Install Suggested Rules** to add all at once. Presets are idempotent: installing again won't create duplicates.

**Setup wizard**

The **Automation & review** step uses the richer per-reason + safeguards flow (see "Dispute routing and automation settings") and lists **every installed template-backed library pack** (draft or active) for per-pack handling. The embedded Rules starter list is a faster path to the same four presets with two actions each.

Rules are evaluated in priority order (portal: reorder on the Rules page). Rule presets are a good starting point; you can edit or delete any rule after installation. Rules require a Starter or Pro plan.

planComparison.title: Comparing Free, Starter, and Pro plans
planComparison.body:
DisputeDesk offers three plans:

Free ($0/month):
- 3 evidence packs per month.
- Manual pack generation only.
- PDF export.
- Save evidence to Shopify.

Starter ($29/month):
- 50 evidence packs per month.
- Auto-pack on dispute sync.
- Custom routing rules.
- PDF export and save to Shopify.
- 7-day free trial.

Pro ($79/month):
- Unlimited evidence packs.
- Auto-pack on dispute sync.
- Custom routing rules.
- Priority support.
- PDF export and save to Shopify.
- 7-day free trial.

All plans include the core features: evidence collection, completeness scoring, PDF export, and saving to Shopify. Paid plans add automation, rules, and higher quotas.

upgradingPlan.title: How to upgrade your plan
upgradingPlan.body:
To upgrade your DisputeDesk plan:

1. Go to the Billing page.
2. Review the plan comparison table.
3. Click "Upgrade to [Plan]" on the plan you want.
4. You'll be redirected to Shopify to approve the recurring charge.
5. After approval, your plan is activated immediately.

Paid plans include a 7-day free trial. You won't be charged until the trial period ends.

If you need to downgrade, contact support. Downgrades take effect at the end of your current billing cycle, and your data is always retained.

Your plan determines your monthly pack quota and which features are available (auto-pack, custom rules, priority support).

**From the marketing site:** Buttons on the public pricing section link to Billing with `?plan=…` so the right tier is selected or the upgrade flow starts. Open DisputeDesk from **Shopify Admin → Apps → DisputeDesk** so your store session is valid; if you are not in the embedded app, you may be prompted to open it from Admin first.

packLimits.title: Understanding pack limits and quotas
packLimits.body:
Each plan has a monthly limit on evidence packs:

- Free: 3 packs per month.
- Starter: 50 packs per month.
- Pro: Unlimited.

The counter resets on the first of each month. You can check your current usage on the Billing page under "Usage This Month."

When you reach your limit:
- New pack generation (manual or automatic) will be blocked.
- A warning banner will appear on the dispute detail page.
- You'll see an "Upgrade Plan" button to increase your quota.

Existing packs are not affected — you can still view, download PDFs, and save completed packs to Shopify even after hitting your limit.

trialPeriod.title: Free trial details
trialPeriod.body:
Both the Starter and Pro plans include a 7-day free trial.

During the trial:
- You have full access to all features of the plan.
- Your pack quota matches the plan level.
- No charge is applied until the trial ends.

After the trial:
- If you keep the plan, billing starts automatically through Shopify.
- If you cancel before the trial ends, you return to the Free plan with no charge.

Trials are managed through Shopify's billing system. You can view your billing status in Shopify Admin > Settings > Billing.

storeSessionUpgrade.title: Can't upgrade: "Store session is invalid"
storeSessionUpgrade.body:
If you see an error like "Store session is invalid (missing shop domain)" or "Reconnect this store to upgrade" when you try to change your plan, DisputeDesk doesn't have a valid connection to your Shopify store for billing.

To fix it:
1. In the sidebar, click **Clear shop & reconnect**.
2. Reconnect your store (enter your store domain and complete the Shopify authorization).
3. Open DisputeDesk from **Shopify Admin** (Apps → DisputeDesk) so the app runs with a valid store session.

The error message on the Billing page includes an **Open in Shopify Admin** link — use it to open your admin and launch the app from there. After that, try upgrading again from the Billing page.

howEvidenceSaved.title: How evidence is saved to Shopify
howEvidenceSaved.body:
When you save evidence, DisputeDesk pushes structured data to Shopify using the disputeEvidenceUpdate GraphQL API.

What gets saved:
- Shipping and tracking documentation (carrier, tracking number, delivery status).
- Customer communication and order details.
- Refund and cancellation policy text.
- Additional file attachments you uploaded.

Each section of your evidence pack is mapped to the appropriate Shopify evidence field. DisputeDesk handles the formatting and field mapping automatically.

After saving:
- The pack status changes to "Saved to Shopify."
- A deep link to Shopify Admin appears so you can review the evidence there.
- An audit event is logged for compliance tracking.

Important: saving evidence to Shopify is NOT the same as submitting to the card network. See "What happens after saving" for next steps.

fieldMapping.title: How evidence fields are mapped to Shopify
fieldMapping.body:
DisputeDesk maps your evidence pack sections to Shopify's dispute evidence fields:

- Shipping & Tracking → shippingDocumentation: carrier info, tracking numbers, delivery proof.
- Customer Details → customerCommunication: customer email, tenure, order history context.
- Refund Policy → refundPolicyDisclosure: your store's refund policy text.
- Cancellation Policy → cancellationPolicyDisclosure: your cancellation terms.
- Order Information → accessActivityLog: order details, line items, amounts.
- Uploaded Files → uncategorizedText: additional documentation you provided.

This mapping happens automatically when you save evidence. You don't need to manually assign fields.

Shopify uses these structured fields when presenting evidence to the card network. The better your evidence maps to the right fields, the stronger your case.

afterSaving.title: What happens after saving evidence
afterSaving.body:
After DisputeDesk saves your evidence to Shopify, here's what happens next:

1. Evidence appears in Shopify Admin: go to Orders > the linked order > Chargeback to review the evidence Shopify received.
2. Review in Shopify: you can add final notes or make adjustments directly in Shopify Admin.
3. Shopify sends evidence to the card network (Visa, Mastercard, etc.) either:
   - When you click the respond button in Shopify Admin, or
   - Automatically on the dispute's due date if you haven't responded manually.
4. Card network reviews: the card network evaluates the evidence and makes a decision.
5. Resolution: the dispute is resolved as "Won" (reversed in your favor) or "Lost."

DisputeDesk monitors the dispute status and updates it when Shopify reports a resolution.

notSubmitToNetworks.title: DisputeDesk does NOT send evidence to card networks
notSubmitToNetworks.body:
This is an important distinction: DisputeDesk saves evidence to Shopify, but does NOT send it onward to card networks (Visa, Mastercard, American Express, etc.).

Here's the process:
1. DisputeDesk builds your evidence pack and saves it to Shopify via API.
2. Forwarding evidence to the card network is handled entirely by Shopify.
3. Shopify either auto-sends on the due date or you respond manually from Shopify Admin.

Why it works this way:
- Shopify is the merchant of record and handles the official communication with card networks.
- This ensures compliance with payment processor requirements.
- Your evidence is formatted and structured by DisputeDesk, then handed off to Shopify for the official process.

You always maintain control. Review your evidence in Shopify Admin before Shopify forwards it to the card network.

embedded.title: Help
embedded.interactiveGuidesDesc: Step-by-step guidance for key tasks in this app.

connectShopifyStore.title: Connecting your store in the Shopify app
connectShopifyStore.body:
When you install DisputeDesk from the Shopify App Store, your store is connected automatically. You're using the app inside Shopify Admin, so no separate "connect" step is needed.

If you need to reconnect (for example after an app update that requests new permissions):
1. Open DisputeDesk from Shopify Admin (Apps → DisputeDesk).
2. If prompted, approve the requested permissions.
3. Your existing data and settings are preserved.

Your store data is encrypted and never shared with third parties.

shopifyAppStoreInstall.title: Using DisputeDesk from the Shopify App Store
shopifyAppStoreInstall.body:
You are using DisputeDesk inside Shopify Admin. That is the recommended way to run the app.

If you have not installed yet:
1. Find DisputeDesk in the Shopify App Store.
2. Tap Add app, approve permissions, and finish install.
3. Open Apps, then DisputeDesk here in Admin.

If you started from our website: complete any OAuth steps Shopify shows, then keep opening this app from Apps (not only from a bookmark) so your session stays valid.

For reconnecting after an update, see Connecting your store in the Shopify app.

understandingDashboard.title: Understanding the dashboard in this app
understandingDashboard.body:
The dashboard gives you an at-a-glance view of your dispute operations and lets you control automation from one place.

**KPI cards**
Four metrics at the top update based on the selected time period (24h / 7d / 30d / All):
- **Active Disputes** — disputes currently open and awaiting a response or under review.
- **Win Rate** — percentage of resolved disputes you won (Won divided by Won + Lost).
- **Evidence Packs** — total evidence packs created for your store.
- **Amount at Risk** — total disputed amount across all currently active disputes.

Where a previous period exists, each card shows a percentage change (green arrow = improved, red arrow = declined).

**Automation Status card**
Shows the four pipeline controls for your store:

- **Auto Build** (ON/OFF) — when a new dispute syncs from Shopify, DisputeDesk automatically creates an evidence pack and starts collecting evidence (order data, tracking info, store policies). If OFF, you click Generate Pack manually on each dispute.

- **Auto Save** (ON/OFF) — once a pack is built and passes the completeness threshold, DisputeDesk automatically saves it to Shopify so it is attached to the dispute. If OFF, you review the pack first and click Save to Shopify manually.

- **Min Score** — the completeness percentage a pack must reach before Auto Save fires. A pack below this score waits until more evidence is collected. You can still save manually below the threshold with a confirmation prompt.

- **Blocker Gate** (ON/OFF) — packs can have blockers: missing critical evidence such as a signed delivery confirmation or a required policy document. When ON, Auto Save is additionally blocked if any blocker is unresolved, regardless of the completeness score. Even a 95% complete pack will not auto-save while a blocker remains.

**Example:** Auto Build ON, Auto Save OFF, Min Score 80%, Blocker Gate ON — DisputeDesk collects evidence automatically for every new dispute, but you review and save each pack manually before it goes to Shopify.

**Recent Disputes table**
Lists your five latest chargebacks. Click the order number to jump directly to that order in Shopify Admin, or click View Details to open the dispute and manage its evidence pack.

afterSaving.title: What happens after saving evidence
afterSaving.body:
After you save evidence from this app to Shopify:

1. Evidence appears in Shopify Admin: go to Orders → the linked order → Chargeback to review what Shopify received.
2. Review in Shopify: you can add final notes or make adjustments in Shopify Admin.
3. Shopify sends evidence to the card network when you click respond in Shopify Admin, or automatically on the dispute's due date.
4. The card network reviews and resolves the dispute as Won or Lost.

DisputeDesk updates the dispute status when Shopify reports the resolution.

viewingFilteringDisputes.title: Viewing and filtering disputes
viewingFilteringDisputes.body:
The **Disputes** page is one card: toolbar (**Search**, **Filter**, **Export**, and **More actions** ⋯ for **Sync now**) and a table. Columns: **Dispute ID** (e.g. DP-6984), **Order** (Shopify Admin link when available), **Reason**, **Amount**, **Status**, **Due date**, and a chevron. **Click any row** to open the dispute; the order link opens Admin and does not navigate away from the list.

Search also matches customer name even though that column is not shown.

There is no separate **Review Queue** tab. Use **Approve & Save** on the detail page when ready.

reviewQueue.title: The review queue
reviewQueue.body:
The review queue is disputes your rules route to **manual review** before evidence is saved to Shopify.

Disputes land here when:
- A rule sends them to review (for example high value).
- No rule matches and the default is review.
- **Require review before auto-save** is on in automation settings.

In the embedded app, those disputes appear on the **Disputes** list with your other chargebacks — use **Filter** by status or open items from the dashboard. Open a dispute to inspect the pack, then use **Approve & Save** on the detail page when ready.

This keeps automation for simple cases and hands unusual or sensitive disputes to you.

approvingDisputes.title: Approving disputes from the review queue
approvingDisputes.body:
Approve from the **dispute detail** page after you open a dispute from the list or dashboard.

Steps:
1. Go to **Disputes** and **click a row** (or open the dispute from the dashboard).
2. Review the evidence pack and completeness on the detail page.
3. Use **Approve & Save** to run the pipeline and save evidence to Shopify when your gates pass.

You can generate or refresh packs on the detail page first. There is no **Approve** button only on the list — the detail page is where you confirm evidence before saving.

onboarding.automationRulesDesc: Set up automation rules to automatically attach evidence packs, send notifications, or flag urgent disputes based on custom conditions.

hero.headline: Run chargebacks like an ops team — not a 25% success-fee tax.
hero.subheadline: DisputeDesk helps you build evidence packs fast, enforce completeness, and keep a defensible audit trail — with merchant-controlled workflows and predictable pricing.

howItWorks.title: How it works
howItWorks.subtitle: Three simple steps to better dispute management
howItWorks.step1Desc: Install from the Shopify App Store. DisputeDesk syncs disputes automatically from that point on.
howItWorks.step2Desc: When a dispute appears, DisputeDesk automatically builds an evidence pack with rules and completeness scoring.
howItWorks.step3Desc: Complete packs are saved to Shopify automatically. Submit in Shopify Admin or let Shopify auto-submit on due date.

security.title: Built for security and compliance
security.subtitle: Your data is protected at every step
security.encryptedDesc: All API keys and access tokens are encrypted at rest with AES-256.
security.auditDesc: Every action is logged with timestamps and user attribution for full traceability.
security.signedDesc: Evidence files are stored with time-limited signed URLs to prevent unauthorized access.

pricing.title: Predictable pricing for dispute ops
pricing.subtitle: Free is for evaluation. Paid plans are for running dispute operations — with clear pack limits and simple top-ups. No hidden percentage.
pricing.trialInfo: Paid plans include a 14-day trial with 25 packs. Free includes 3 packs total (lifetime).
pricing.topUpsDesc: Purchase top-up bundles any time. Credits are added immediately upon payment.

rules.title: How new disputes are handled
rules.subtitle: Choose how each active pack should behave when a new dispute arrives.
rules.packHandlingIntro: Choose how each active pack should behave when a new dispute arrives.
rules.packHandlingHelper: Packs you activated in the previous step appear here automatically. Set whether each one should run automatically or go through manual review first.
rules.segmentAutoHint: Creates and routes the pack automatically
rules.packAutoDisabledHint: This pack needs an installed template to enable automatic handling.
rules.emptyPacksBody: Activate packs in the previous step to configure rules here.
rules.pack_auto_requires_template: Automatic handling requires a template linked to this pack.
rules.introOneLiner: Templates define what goes into a pack. This step defines what happens when a new dispute arrives.
rules.heroIntro: When a dispute arrives, DisputeDesk uses your default, any reason-specific rule, then safeguards — in that order.
rules.packsRelationBody: Pack templates in your library define sections and evidence checklists. Automation rules here decide what happens when a dispute first syncs — auto-build a pack, send it to review first, or leave it manual.
rules.packsPrereqBody: To assign pack templates and set auto-build or review behavior by dispute reason, install at least one pack from the setup step.
rules.rulesTableLockedAria: Dispute reason rules are disabled until you install at least one pack template.
rules.presetManualLine: You prepare evidence yourself for each dispute.
rules.presetReviewLine: DisputeDesk gathers data, then you review before submitting.
rules.presetAutoLine: We build the pack automatically where it’s safe (when templates are installed).
rules.defaultSectionSubtitle: Used when no exception below matches the dispute reason.
rules.exceptionsSectionSubtitle: Optional — only change reasons that should behave differently from your default.
rules.safeguardsOverrideNote: When on, these take priority over your default and per-reason choices above.
rules.summaryCatchAll: When no other rule matches: send to review
rules.reasonHelpPnr: Customer says the order never arrived.
rules.reasonHelpUnacceptable: Item differs from listing or is defective.
rules.reasonHelpCredit: Refund or store credit not received.
rules.reasonHelpGeneral: Everything else that doesn’t fit the reasons above.
rules.noTemplatesInstalled: Install packs from the library first
rules.precedenceNote: Amount-based safeguards are evaluated first, then rules for the dispute reason, then catch-all rules. If multiple rules could apply at the same tier, review overrides auto-build.
rules.fallbackNote: If no rule applies, the dispute stays manual (no automatic pack build).
rules.safeguardsIntro: These override the reason-based handling above.
rules.catchAllLabel: Send to review when no specific rule applies
rules.load_failed: Could not load automation settings. Refresh and try again.
rules.validationAutoBuildTemplate: Select a pack template for every reason set to Auto-build.
rules.validationTemplateInstalled: Choose a template from your installed packs library.
rules.changeLaterBody: These settings can be changed anytime in Rules.
rules.activatedPackagesListIntro: These evidence packs are ACTIVE in your library (from the Evidence packs step).
rules.activatedPackagesEmpty: No activated packs yet. Install a template from the packs step and activate it to see it here.
